skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posts, likes, retweets, DMs, and follows via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. Static findings are primarily false positives. The HIGH flags for weak cryptographic algorithms at lines 3 and 27 incorrectly flag YAML frontmatter and table syntax. The 36 MEDIUM external_command flags are bash CLI examples in code blocks with hardcoded arguments. The 7 LOW hardcoded URL flags point to legitimate inference.sh domains. No confirmed malicious patterns or prompt injection detected. Skill is safe for publication.

high_findings.title: Incorrect Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and :27. These are FALSE POSITIVES. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter metadata, line 27 is table header syntax. No cryptographic code exists in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations contain YAML metadata and table syntax, not cryptographic code. Pattern matching incorrectly triggered on benign text patterns.

medium_findings.title: CLI Command Examples Flagged as External Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 36 locations as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are FALSE POSITIVES. All flagged locations are 'belt app run' commands inside code blocks with hardcoded string arguments. No command injection risk exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged commands use hardcoded JSON strings with no user input. This is legitimate CLI documentation showing example usage patterns.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to Service Domain
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 7 hardcoded URLs to inference.sh domains. These are TRUE POSITIVES but low severity. URLs point to the service's own documentation and CDN assets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs link to legitimate inference.sh service infrastructure. Expected behavior for service documentation.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter/X posts, likes, and follows
content.value_statement: Managing social media accounts manually takes time. This skill automates Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user interactions via a simple CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets with custom content
Post tweets with image or video media attachments
Like and retweet posts by tweet ID
Send direct messages to other users
Follow and unfollow Twitter users by username
Retrieve user profiles and tweet details by ID
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) and account authentication
Cannot access tweets or users without their ID or username
Rate limits depend on X API tier and account permissions

use_cases.title: Social media manager scheduling content
use_cases.description: Create and schedule tweets with images or videos for brand accounts. Automate routine posts without manual login.

use_cases.title: Developer building AI-powered bots
use_cases.description: Integrate tweet creation into AI workflows. Post AI-generated images or videos automatically after creation.

use_cases.title: Community manager engaging with followers
use_cases.description: Automate thank-you DMs to new followers and like posts from specific users to boost engagement.

prompt_templates.title: Post a simple text tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet that says: {tweet_text}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to post a text-only tweet

prompt_templates.title: Post a tweet with an image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet with image. Text: {text}, Image URL: {image_url}
prompt_templates.scenario: When posting content with visual media

prompt_templates.title: Engage with a tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to like and retweet the tweet with ID {tweet_id}

prompt_templates.title: Send a welcome message
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to follow {username} and send them a DM saying: {message}

output_examples.input: Post a tweet announcing our new feature launch
output_examples.output:
Successfully posted tweet: 'Excited to announce our new feature! Check it out at example.com 🚀'

Tweet ID: 1234567890
Created at: 2026-05-07T10:30:00Z

output_examples.input: Like and retweet the announcement from @OpenAI
output_examples.output:
Liked tweet ID 9876543210
Retweeted successfully
Action completed at: 2026-05-07T10:31:00Z

output_examples.input: Follow @anthropicai and send them a thank you DM
output_examples.output:
Followed @anthropicai
Direct message sent: 'Thanks for the great work on Claude! 💪'
Actions completed successfully

best_practices: Test commands with the sample flag before running live operations
Use JSON input files for complex posts with media to avoid escaping issues
Store credentials securely and never share API tokens publicly
anti_patterns: Do not post duplicate content across multiple accounts without disclosure
Avoid high-frequency actions that may trigger X API rate limits
Do not use this skill to spam, harass, or violate X platform terms of service

faq.question: How do I set up authentication for Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Run 'belt login' to authenticate with your inference.sh account. You need X API credentials configured in your inference.sh dashboard.

faq.question: Can I post images or videos with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use the x/post-create app with a media_url field. Supported formats include JPG, PNG, GIF, and MP4.

faq.question: What happens if my tweet exceeds character limits?
faq.answer: X API will return an error. The skill passes the error message back so you can adjust your content.

faq.question: How do I find a tweet or user ID?
faq.answer: Use x/post-get with the tweet URL or x/user-get with the username. You can also get IDs from X's own interface or third-party tools.

faq.question: Are there rate limits I should be aware of?
faq.answer: Yes, X API enforces rate limits. The limits depend on your API tier. The skill does not implement rate limit handling, so add delays if needed.

faq.question: Can I delete tweets I previously posted?
faq.answer: Yes, use x/post-delete with the tweet_id. Note: you can only delete your own tweets.
